operating_state: ConocoPhillips generated total revenues of $55.6 billion in 2022, driven by WTI crude averaging $94 per barrel versus $68 in 2021 and production reaching 1,738 MBOED, up 11 percent year-over-year from Shell Permian acquisition and new wells across Lower 48, Alaska, Australia, China, Malaysia and Canada. After adjusting for acquisitions and dispositions, organic production declined 1 percent due to fourth quarter weather impacts and downtime.
cost_structure: Production and operating expenses increased $1.3 billion due to higher volumes from Shell Permian acquisition, inflation and commodity price impacts, while DD&A expenses rose from higher production volumes. Capital expenditures increased significantly to $10.2 billion in 2022 from $5.3 billion in 2021 across all segments. Environmental compliance costs reached $705 million with projected increases driven by new EPA GHG regulations and Inflation Reduction Act methane emission charges.
material_events: ConocoPhillips completed $3.3 billion debt reduction through early retirements and refinancing toward $5 billion target by 2026, reducing total debt to $16.6 billion while maintaining A-range credit ratings. The company returned $15 billion to shareholders including share repurchases and dividends, with Board increasing repurchase authorization from $25 billion to $45 billion. Expanded LNG business through Qatar NFE and NFS projects interest and additional APLNG interest to 47.5 percent.
macro_exposures: Operations face heightened geopolitical risks from armed hostilities in oil-producing regions, international sanctions, and Ukraine conflict impacts including Australia's natural gas price cap implemented in December 2022. Climate litigation has increased with cities and governments filing lawsuits since 2017 seeking compensatory damages. Climate-related regulatory costs increased with EU ETS compliance, UK ETS, Norwegian carbon legislation fees, and evolving EPA methane standards.
forward_outlook: Management announced 2023 capital expenditure guidance of $10.7 to $11.3 billion with production guidance of 1.76 to 1.80 MMBOED. The company committed to $11 billion in 2023 shareholder returns and published net-zero operational emissions ambition by 2050. Management targets $5 billion total debt reduction by end-2026 and established near-zero 2030 methane emissions intensity target.
uncertainty_profile: Reserve estimates remain subject to substantial risk from governmental regulation changes, commodity price volatility, and technological assumptions, with reserve replacement dependent on price cycles affecting project economics. Development projects remain subject to completing commercial agreements, resolving risks, obtaining financing and reaching final investment decisions. Cybersecurity threats pose growing risks to operational systems, and net-zero transition success depends on undeveloped technologies and policies beyond company control.
